import pandas as pd
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import os
import numpy as np
# first get into each trial folder
trial_number = 8
print(os.listdir("."))   
# format for trial folder is two digit number with leading zero if necessary
trial_folder = os.path.join("Subject_1/Baseline (with_audio_and_visual_stimulus)",f"trial_{trial_number:02d}")

In [58]:
#05:01:13.064946 -> time format
#find statistics of how the timestep varies from index to index
trial_data['timestamp'] = pd.to_datetime(trial_data['timestamp'], format='%H:%M:%S.%f')
time_diffs = pd.to_datetime(trial_data['timestamp']).diff().dt.total_seconds().dropna()
print("Min:", time_diffs.min(), "seconds")
print("Max:", time_diffs.max(), "seconds")
print("Std:", time_diffs.std(), "seconds")
print("Mean:", time_diffs.mean(), "seconds")

#number of samples totally
print("Total samples:", len(trial_data))

#estimated time
estimated_time = time_diffs.sum()
print("Estimated time duration of trial:", estimated_time, "seconds")

# estimated sampling rate for fourier analysis
sampling_rate = 1 / time_diffs.mean()
print("Estimated sampling rate:", sampling_rate, "Hz")
estimated_sampling_rate_time = time_diffs.mean()*3000

# error check between assumed sampling rate and actual time duration
actual_duration = (trial_data['timestamp'].iloc[-1] - trial_data['timestamp'].iloc[0]).total_seconds()
print("Actual time duration of trial:", actual_duration, "seconds")
time_error = abs(actual_duration - estimated_sampling_rate_time)
print("Time duration error:", time_error, "seconds")

Min: 0.011 seconds
Max: 0.027663 seconds
Std: 0.0007283277084557502 seconds
Mean: 0.01153043281093698 seconds
Total samples: 3000
Estimated time duration of trial: 34.579768 seconds
Estimated sampling rate: 86.72701332177822 Hz
Actual time duration of trial: 34.579768 seconds
Time duration error: 0.01153043281093602 seconds
In [59]:
# using the sampling rate, plot the eeg data of one channel against time
time_axis = np.arange(len(trial_data)) / sampling_rate

plt.figure(figsize=(12, 6))
# plt.plot(time_axis, trial_data['eeg2'], label="eeg")
for i in range(len(trial_data.columns)-2):
    plt.plot(time_axis, trial_data.iloc[:, i+1], label=trial_data.columns[i+1])

plt.xlabel("Time (s)")
plt.ylabel("EEG Signal")
plt.title("EEG Data - 19 Channels (20 ignored as 0)")
plt.legend()

# ticks every 1 second till 30
plt.xticks(np.arange(0, 31, 1))
plt.xlim(0, 30)

plt.grid(True, alpha=0.3)
plt.show()

Linear Regression Test (Time Series)¶

In [60]:
# Reconstruct channels 2 and 7 from all other channels + evaluation metrics
from sklearn.metrics import r2_score, mean_squared_error
from sklearn.linear_model import Ridge
# Channel 2
X_ch2 = trial_data.iloc[:, [i + 1 for i in range(19) if i + 1 != 2]]  # all except eeg2
y_ch2 = trial_data["eeg2"].values
model_ch2 = Ridge(alpha=1.0)
model_ch2.fit(X_ch2, y_ch2)
y_pred_ch2 = model_ch2.predict(X_ch2)

r2_ch2 = r2_score(y_ch2, y_pred_ch2)
mse_ch2 = mean_squared_error(y_ch2, y_pred_ch2)

# Channel 7
X_ch7 = trial_data.iloc[:, [i + 1 for i in range(19) if i + 1 != 7]]  # all except eeg7
y_ch7 = trial_data["eeg7"].values
model_ch7 = Ridge(alpha=1.0)
model_ch7.fit(X_ch7, y_ch7)
y_pred_ch7 = model_ch7.predict(X_ch7)

r2_ch7 = r2_score(y_ch7, y_pred_ch7)
mse_ch7 = mean_squared_error(y_ch7, y_pred_ch7)

print(f"eeg2 -> R²: {r2_ch2:.6f}, MSE: {mse_ch2:.6f}")
print(f"eeg7 -> R²: {r2_ch7:.6f}, MSE: {mse_ch7:.6f}")

# Plot both reconstructions
fig, axes = plt.subplots(2, 1, figsize=(12, 8))

# Channel 2
axes[0].plot(time_axis, y_ch2, label="Actual eeg2", alpha=0.7)
axes[0].plot(time_axis, y_pred_ch2, label="Predicted eeg2", alpha=0.7)
axes[0].set_xlabel("Time (s)")
axes[0].set_ylabel("EEG Signal")
axes[0].set_title(f"Ridge Prediction of eeg2 (R²={r2_ch2:.4f}, MSE={mse_ch2:.4f})")
axes[0].legend()
axes[0].grid(True, alpha=0.3)

# Channel 7
axes[1].plot(time_axis, y_ch7, label="Actual eeg7", alpha=0.7)
axes[1].plot(time_axis, y_pred_ch7, label="Predicted eeg7", alpha=0.7)
axes[1].set_xlabel("Time (s)")
axes[1].set_ylabel("EEG Signal")
axes[1].set_title(f"Ridge Prediction of eeg7 (R²={r2_ch7:.4f}, MSE={mse_ch7:.4f})")
axes[1].legend()
axes[1].grid(True, alpha=0.3)

plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()

eeg2 -> R²: 0.999967, MSE: 0.006368
eeg7 -> R²: 0.999978, MSE: 0.006379

TimeSeries Conclusion: Baseline EEG data is almost indistinguishable from each channel.¶

Inputs: Data could be biased or ground electrode may not be connected. This could just be static noise¶

Action: Check the software if reference electrode and montage is properly configured.¶

Time-Frequency Analysis (STFT, Wavelet, and Welch PSD)¶

In [61]:
# ── STFT (Short-Time Fourier Transform) Spectrogram ──
from scipy.signal import stft

# pick a single channel to visualise (change ch_idx as needed)
ch_idx = 1
ch_name = trial_data.columns[ch_idx]
signal = trial_data.iloc[:, ch_idx].values.astype(float)
signal = signal - np.mean(signal)

nperseg  = int(2 * sampling_rate)      # 2-second window
noverlap = int(1.5 * sampling_rate)    # 75 % overlap

f_stft, t_stft, Zxx = stft(
    signal,
    fs=sampling_rate,
    nperseg=nperseg,
    noverlap=noverlap,
)

plt.figure(figsize=(14, 6))
plt.pcolormesh(
    t_stft, f_stft, np.abs(Zxx),
    shading='gouraud', cmap='inferno'
)
plt.colorbar(label='|STFT Magnitude|')
plt.ylabel('Frequency (Hz)')
plt.xlabel('Time (s)')
plt.title(f'STFT Spectrogram – {ch_name}')
plt.ylim(0, 60)          # EEG bands live below ~50 Hz
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
In [62]:
# ── STFT Spectrograms for ALL EEG Channels (subplot grid) ──
from scipy.signal import stft

n_channels = len(trial_data.columns) - 2   # exclude timestamp & last col
cols = 4
rows = int(np.ceil(n_channels / cols))

fig, axes = plt.subplots(rows, cols, figsize=(20, 3 * rows), sharex=True, sharey=True)
axes = axes.flatten()

nperseg  = int(2 * sampling_rate)
noverlap = int(1.5 * sampling_rate)

for i in range(n_channels):
    ch_name = trial_data.columns[i + 1]
    sig = trial_data.iloc[:, i + 1].values.astype(float)
    sig = sig - np.mean(sig)

    f_s, t_s, Zxx = stft(sig, fs=sampling_rate, nperseg=nperseg, noverlap=noverlap)

    axes[i].pcolormesh(t_s, f_s, np.abs(Zxx), shading='gouraud', cmap='inferno')
    axes[i].set_ylim(0, 60)
    axes[i].set_title(ch_name, fontsize=9)

# hide unused subplots
for j in range(n_channels, len(axes)):
    axes[j].set_visible(False)

fig.supxlabel('Time (s)')
fig.supylabel('Frequency (Hz)')
fig.suptitle('STFT Spectrograms – All EEG Channels', fontsize=14)
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()

Continuous Wavelet Transform (CWT)¶

Uses a Morlet wavelet to produce a scalogram — better frequency resolution at low frequencies and better time resolution at high frequencies compared to STFT.

In [67]:
# ── Continuous Wavelet Transform (single channel) ──
import pywt

ch_idx = 2
ch_name = trial_data.columns[ch_idx]
signal = trial_data.iloc[:, ch_idx].values.astype(float)
signal = signal - np.mean(signal)

# frequencies of interest: 1 – 60 Hz (logarithmically spaced)
freqs_of_interest = np.logspace(np.log10(1), np.log10(60), num=80)

# compute scales: scale = central_freq / (freq * dt)
wavelet = 'cmor1.5-1.0'
central_freq = pywt.central_frequency(wavelet)
dt = 1.0 / sampling_rate
scales = central_freq / (freqs_of_interest * dt)

coefficients, frequencies = pywt.cwt(signal, scales, wavelet, sampling_period=dt)
power = np.abs(coefficients) ** 2

time_axis_cwt = np.arange(len(signal)) / sampling_rate

plt.figure(figsize=(14, 6))
plt.pcolormesh(
    time_axis_cwt, freqs_of_interest, power,
    shading='gouraud', cmap='jet'
)
plt.colorbar(label='Power')
plt.yscale('log')
plt.ylabel('Frequency (Hz)')
plt.xlabel('Time (s)')
plt.title(f'Continuous Wavelet Transform (Morlet) – {ch_name}')
plt.ylim(1, 60)
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
In [64]:
# ── CWT Scalograms for ALL EEG Channels ──
import pywt

n_channels = len(trial_data.columns) - 2
cols = 4
rows = int(np.ceil(n_channels / cols))

freqs_of_interest = np.logspace(np.log10(1), np.log10(60), num=60)
wavelet = 'cmor1.5-1.0'
central_freq = pywt.central_frequency(wavelet)
dt = 1.0 / sampling_rate
scales = central_freq / (freqs_of_interest * dt)
time_axis_cwt = np.arange(len(trial_data)) / sampling_rate

fig, axes = plt.subplots(rows, cols, figsize=(20, 3 * rows), sharex=True, sharey=True)
axes = axes.flatten()

for i in range(n_channels):
    ch_name = trial_data.columns[i + 1]
    sig = trial_data.iloc[:, i + 1].values.astype(float)
    sig = sig - np.mean(sig)

    coefficients, frequencies = pywt.cwt(sig, scales, wavelet, sampling_period=dt)
    power = np.abs(coefficients) ** 2

    axes[i].pcolormesh(time_axis_cwt, freqs_of_interest, power,
                       shading='gouraud', cmap='jet')
    axes[i].set_yscale('log')
    axes[i].set_ylim(1, 60)
    axes[i].set_title(ch_name, fontsize=9)

for j in range(n_channels, len(axes)):
    axes[j].set_visible(False)

fig.supxlabel('Time (s)')
fig.supylabel('Frequency (Hz)')
fig.suptitle('CWT Scalograms (Morlet) – All EEG Channels', fontsize=14)
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
In [65]:
from scipy.signal import welch

plt.figure(figsize=(12, 6))

# assuming eeg channels start from column index 1 and end before last 1-2 cols
for i in range(len(trial_data.columns) - 2):
    ch_name = trial_data.columns[i+1]
    signal = trial_data.iloc[:, i+1].values

    # remove DC
    signal = signal - np.mean(signal)

    # Welch PSD
    freqs, psd = welch(
        signal,
        fs=sampling_rate,
        nperseg=4 * sampling_rate,   # 4-second window
        noverlap=2 * sampling_rate   # 50% overlap
    )

    plt.semilogy(freqs, psd, label=ch_name)

plt.xlabel("Frequency (Hz)")
plt.ylabel("PSD (V²/Hz)")
plt.title("Welch Power Spectral Density (EEG Channels)")
plt.xlim(1, sampling_rate/2)   # EEG usually meaningful up to 50 Hz
plt.grid(True, alpha=0.3)
plt.legend()
plt.show()

Fourier Series also looks similar for all channels. Check if reference electrode and montage is properly configured.¶
